creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1: Software Factories & Keynotes
videos.summary: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1 livestream from Moscone West in San Francisco on June 30, 2026 features keynotes and main-stage programming on software factories and AI engineering. The event brings together one of the largest gatherings of AI engineers for live talks and presentations.

videos.title: Building Great Agent Skills: The Missing Manual
videos.summary: A framework for building high-quality AI agent skills by addressing "skill hell" through structured design. The approach covers five key dimensions: Trigger (user-invoked vs model-invoked), Structure (steps and reference organization), Steering (leading words to guide agent behavior), increased task complexity per step, and Pruning (removing legacy material and no-ops).

videos.title: Frontier results, on device - RL Nabors, Arize
videos.summary: RL Nabors argues that frontier models are overused due to cost and latency, and many calls could be handled by small local models instead. The talk introduces evaluation vocabulary and frameworks for measuring capability to make informed model selection decisions based on production data rather than benchmark scores.

videos.title: The Future Is Domain-Specific Agents - Justin Schroeder, StandardAgents
videos.summary: Justin Schroeder argues that domain-specific agents composed together represent a superior architecture to skills-based systems, enabling smaller models to perform real work and driving AI from internal tools to customer-facing products. The talk advocates for composition-over-inheritance as the foundational principle for useful AI systems.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Anthropic Just Released Claude Sonnet 5 and it's VERY IMPRESSIVE!
videos.summary: Cole Medin reviews Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 5 as an impressive model offering excellent value. The video frames the model as a significant advancement and part of broader AI transformation across industries.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin reviews Google's whitepaper on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model is only 10% of success while the harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) comprises the other 90%. The video outlines a spectrum from vibe coding to production agentic engineering, emphasizing context engineering, the shift from conductor to orchestrator roles, and token economics as key considerations for AI-driven software development in 2026.

videos.title: My AI Memory Now Follows Me Across Every Tool!
videos.summary: Walrus Memory is a memory layer that persists outside individual AI tools, enabling context continuity across different AI agents and providers. It stores encrypted memories owned by users and allows selective access control between agents.

videos.title: The New Kimi K2.7 Model is VERY Impressive
videos.summary: Kimi K2.7 is an open-source, coding-focused agentic model developed by Moonshot AI. The model is described as impressively capable relative to its size and cost.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: I was giving my coding agent context the wrong way...
videos.summary: Video covers best practices for providing context to coding agents. Discusses codebase harness setup and demonstrates techniques for improving agent performance through proper context structuring.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason announces a new project related to OpenClaw, referencing Crabbox and Codebase harness tools. The GitHub repository contains skills for setup, with accompanying educational resources through AI Builder Club and Superdesign platform.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason explains Loop Engineer, a tool for setting up AI agents. The video provides a GitHub template repository with a codebase harness and knowledge template, plus a workshop in the AI Builder Club for hands-on learning.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving company systems through autonomous loops. Covers use cases in ads optimization and SEO, featuring Loopany framework with memory and tool integration.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: GPT 5.6 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code reviews OpenAI's announcement of GPT 5.6, noting government regulation as the AI industry matures. The video compares OpenAI's consolidated model lineup (Sol, Terra, Luna) with Anthropic's models (Mythos 5, Fable 5), which faced regulatory pause.

videos.title: It's about time we learn Transformers..
videos.summary: A video tutorial explaining the mechanics of Transformer architecture and GPT models. Covers self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, positional embeddings, and layer normalization as the core components enabling generative pre-trained transformers.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: A video tutorial explaining Andrej Karpathy's bigram language model approach as a foundational step toward understanding GPT and transformers. The tutorial covers tokenization, embedding, loss functions, backpropagation, and optimization techniques essential to understanding how language models process tokens.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series comprising Nano, Super, and Ultra models. The architecture combines hybrid Mamba transformer, Mixture of Tokens Prediction (MTP), and Latent Mixture of Experts (MoE) approaches to distinguish itself from other open models.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Containers on Amazon ECS with Mama J
videos.summary: AWS Developers' educational video explains containerization concepts and how Amazon ECS orchestrates containers at scale. The content covers Docker image building, ECS as an orchestrator, Fargate vs EC2 deployment options, and practical auto-scaling examples.

videos.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
videos.summary: A video tutorial introducing a 7-step system design framework for beginners that prioritizes understanding requirements before technology selection. The framework covers requirements gathering, traffic estimation, high-level components, API contracts, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints applied to a URL shortener example.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: The video explains how AI agents use tool calling to move beyond static language models by integrating external tools that can access real-time data and execute actions. It covers tool architecture including schemas, descriptions, and handlers, plus how system prompts direct agent tool selection.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS equips AI coding agents with tools, documentation, best practices, and guardrails for working with AWS services. It provides up-to-date information and tested procedures to improve agent success rates and reduce inefficiencies.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Why is AI expensive all of a sudden?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech examines why AI inference has become expensive, focusing on the trend of "tokenmaxxing" where models are deliberately padded with extra tokens to increase billing. The video references industry analysis from The Pragmatic Engineer blog and explores the rise and fall of this practice and underlying cost drivers.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A video discussing whether Google engineers use AI-assisted code generation ("vibing code"), comparing approaches from OpenAI and Claude, and examining Google's practices across 2024-2026. The content explores what counts as vibe coding and Google's position on the practice.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech examines Malus, a company or tool positioned as disruptive to open source, discussing its implications for open source maintainers and the ecosystem. The video frames the topic as a provocative debate about whether Malus represents an existential threat to open source.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: He Built $100B at Google, Then Failed. Now He Runs an $80B+ Company | Snowflake, Sridhar Ramaswamy
videos.summary: Sridhar Ramaswamy, CEO of Snowflake, shares lessons from his tenure scaling Google's advertising business, his failed search engine startup Neeva, and his approach to leading Snowflake into the AI era. He emphasizes that founders and leaders must remain close to product development, hire adaptable teams, and recognize that both failures and successes do not define their identity.

videos.title: Everything You Should Know Before Mythos Arrives | Nebulock, Damien Lewke
videos.summary: Damien Lewke argues that AI has collapsed the cost of cyberattacks from requiring elite teams and a decade of expertise to just two people and a GPU. He advocates an "assume breach" posture as the only realistic defense strategy and outlines three behavioral signals that reveal attackers already inside networks, emphasizing that defenders must shift from reactive alerting to proactive threat hunting.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that code alone is not a software moat; competitive advantage comes from customer context, proprietary data, product experience, and operational scale. He contends that fears of AI destroying SaaS companies are overblown and that the best AI products succeed by creating raving fans through strong customer obsession.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a coming "ChatGPT moment" in robotics will bring billions of robots, but the focus on humanoid form factors by companies like 1X and Unitree is myopic. Design should prioritize human interaction and delight rather than utility alone.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI vs. Dedicated Software: The Real Efficiency Debate #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof questions whether integrating AI for general-purpose tasks delivers long-term efficiency gains or represents a costly mistake compared to purpose-built software. The video explores potential drawbacks of widespread AI adoption.

videos.title: Germany Heatwave MELTED my Ice Cream in Lisbon! #shorts
videos.summary: A short-form video documents extreme heat affecting cities like Germany and Lisbon, attributing intensified urban temperatures to the urban heat island effect. The content frames rising heat as part of broader climate change impacts on city environments.

videos.title: AI Learns, Takes Initiative, Works Asynchronously: The Future is Here! #shorts
videos.summary: A video claims that AI systems learn, take initiative, and operate asynchronously in ambient mode, demonstrating organizational learning capabilities. The creator positions this pattern as representing the future of AI technology.

videos.title: Claude Tag: AI's European Takeover in Microsoft Teams! #shorts
videos.summary: A YouTube short by Cyril Imhof discusses Claude Tag integration into Microsoft Teams and Slack, questioning whether this represents a sophisticated marketing play targeting enterprise adoption. The piece explores strategic positioning of AI in workplace communication platforms.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
